gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/286)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (11/286)
text: قَالَ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) الْمُسْتَتِرُ بِالْحَسَنَةِ يَعْدِلُ سَبْعِينَ حَسَنَةً وَ الْمُذِيعُ بِالسَّيِّئَةِ مَخْذُولٌ وَ الْمُسْتَتِرُ بِهَا مَغْفُورٌ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: Muhammad Bin yahya, from Muhammad Bin Sandal, from Yasser, from Al Yas’a Bin Hamza,

(It has been narrated) from Al-Reza<sup>asws</sup> having said: ‘Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup> said: ‘The veiling of the good deeds equates to seventy good deeds, and the broadcaster of the evil deeds is rebuked, and he would be Forgiven who hides (his sins)’.
en.sarwar: 2. Muhammad ibn Yahya has narrated from Muhammad ibn Sandal from Yasir from al-Yasa’ ibn Hamza from al-Rida (a.s.) who has said the following: “The Messenger of Allah has said, ‘To keep a good deed unnoticed is equal to seventy good deeds. Publicizing an evil deed brings humiliation and for its concealment one may receive forgiveness.’”
